summary_one_line: A sanitization-passing OpenType name table with a non-zero NameRecord count so all_names.length > 0 and the loop body executes.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A name-table header whose count/stringOffset fields and trailing NameRecord array survive hb_sanitize_context_t so that all_names.length (= this->table->count) is non-zero; the structured header tags ('typ1', 'name', table fourccs like 'GB') must match the values the parser compares against.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The VPC winner-resolving seed begins with structured header bytes 74 79 70 31 ('typ1') and contains 'name' and a fourcc-like 'GB' run, and resolves the branch (resolved=9/10) hitting the loop body 13 times on average; its lineage carries an I2S-floor dash row, evidence that I2SRandReplace substituted a logged CMP operand (a magic/fourcc constant) into the header so sanitization passed.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser blocks 10/10 with avg hitcount 0 — its seeds never produce a sanitization-passing non-zero count; value_profile has no I2SRandReplace, so it cannot copy the exact compared header constant into the input and stays stuck at the false (zero-length) side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog has the I2SRandReplace mutator that reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the other operand of an intercepted integer/string CMP at matching offsets, planting the exact header constant the name-table sanitizer demands; plain value_profile lacks this mutator, so this pair's win is attributable to I2S.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Incrementally improving header bytes that approach a valid count/offset relationship; the CMP_MAP gradient rewards inputs whose compared operands get closer to matching, providing intermediate corpus stepping stones toward a sanitization-passing non-zero NameRecord count.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The VPC winner resolves 9/10 vs cmplog's 1/10 and reaches 13 hits vs 2; VPC adds the CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback on top of cmplog's I2S, so partial-match header states are preserved as corpus and combined with I2S substitution to clear the sanitizer.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks 9/10 with avg hitcount only 2 — it has I2S but no CMP_MAP gradient, so near-miss header inputs that improve operand-distance are not retained as new coverage and the search has no gradient toward a valid non-zero-count table.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP records (PC, operand-distance bucket); an input whose CMP operands are closer to matching than any seen adds a new bucket and is kept as corpus, giving a Hamming/prefix-distance gradient toward a passing header that plain cmplog (no CMP_MAP) lacks; this pair's win is attributable to value_profile.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side is the loop body guarded by all_names.length, which must be non-zero to execute.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: for (unsigned int i = 0; i < all_names.length; i++) <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: all_names.length comes from this->table->count read from the parsed name-table header.
The table is obtained through hb_sanitize_context_t, so the header must pass sanitization before count is usable.
VPC beats value_profile with the I2S technique delta.
The VPC winner resolves 9/10 and the value_profile loser blocks 10/10 with zero hits on the branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=13  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-resolving seed begins with structured header bytes spelling 'typ1' and 'name'.
I2SRandReplace substitutes a logged CMP operand at matching offsets in the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: VPC beats cmplog with the value_profile technique delta.
Against cmplog the VPC winner reaches 13 hits while cmplog reaches only 2.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=13  loser=2

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile keeps as corpus any input whose CMP operands get closer to matching, giving a distance gradient.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog combines the CMP_MAP gradient buckets with cmplog's per-execution CMP buffer.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5250 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 2 --seed 55e02621633263d5` and observed exactly 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 49 of the 50-level ancestor chain, the lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current vpc build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose loop bound is gated on a name-table count field shows value_profile resolving the branch as often as value_profile_cmplog (no I2S advantage), which would refute that I2S substitution of the header constant is what clears the gate.

weakest_evidence_point: The attribution of pair 2's win specifically to the CMP_MAP gradient is inferential — the prompt shows VPC>cmplog with a value_profile delta but no direct byte-level evidence of an intermediate near-match corpus chain.
